01
Impact

Our Impact

Deloitte Digital was engaged by a government department to design a centralised platform and series of tools supporting primary industry workers in their day-to-day activities during a natural disaster — enabling better decisions, faster preparation, and a faster return to normal.

57K+
Primary industry workers supported across the platform
↑ FARM
Recognised by CDNA — connecting primary producers to support services across Australia
3
Core design goals: confident daily decisions, proactive preparation, and resilience
Final DesignProduction dashboard — farming disaster relief support platform
02
Current State

The Problem

A library of farming decision tools exists in the current state, however users are required to input large amounts of data each session that cannot be retained or re-used. The current tools are overly complex and their use-cases are not clearly framed.

📋
Low Tool Literacy
Farmers find it difficult to start or to understand whether their tool is correct for their needs. Help text is minimal and on-boarding is not designed for low-frequency, high-stress use.
🔁
No Data Persistence
Data input cannot be saved, shared, or retrieved between sessions. Users re-enter the same information repeatedly, adding unnecessary cognitive load during already stressful situations.
🧩
Fragmented Ecosystem
Accountability requirements, tool inconsistencies, and unclear updates to billing/reporting make the current system hard to trust. No single source of truth exists for farmers or government administrators.
03
Design Goals

Objectives

Goal 01
Simplify the experience for farmers with decision-making activities
Re-scope and simplify each tool so farmers can immediately understand what it does, what data they need, and what the output means for their situation.
Goal 02
Enable seamless access in disrupted conditions
Design a better platform so farmers can access tools quickly, accurately, and consistently — even with intermittent connectivity or high cognitive load during a disaster event.
Goal 03
Accelerate development within existing complexity
Build a design system and componentised output that engineering can implement rapidly and maintain confidently, reducing rework and accelerating delivery of future tools.
Goal 04
Federate internal development and publishing
Establish shared libraries and governance so the department can create, update, and publish new tools independently — without ongoing agency dependency.
04
How We Worked

Process

Phase 1
User Identification & Research
  • User identification and documentation
  • Contextual interviews with primary industry workers
  • Stakeholder workshops
  • Current state tool audit
  • Journey mapping
Phase 2
Information Architecture
  • Priority user flows
  • Account management model
  • Capabilities matrix
  • Sitemap definition
  • Wireframes & specifications
Phase 3
High-Fidelity Prototype Design
  • Component library build
  • Dashboard iterations (×2)
  • Moderated usability testing
  • Accessibility review
  • Final design & handoff
05
Research & Architecture

Priority User Flows & Sitemap

We mapped end-to-end journeys for the platform's primary user types — primary industry workers across livestock, horticulture, and mixed enterprises. Account management emerged as the critical foundation: users needed to input their land and business context once and have it persist across all tools.

The capabilities matrix defined which tools a user could access based on their declared context — feeding directly into the sitemap and driving the navigation hierarchy of the final platform.

User ResearchPriority user flow — account management and cross-tool data persistence model
IAPlatform sitemap — tool hierarchy, access permissions, and navigation structure
06
Detailed Design

Wireframes & Specifications

Wireframes moved through three fidelity levels — concept layouts, annotated lo-fi, and component-level specs. Each stage was reviewed with the government client and engineering delivery team before progressing, ensuring alignment on technical feasibility and compliance requirements throughout.

WireframesDashboard layout explorations — query form, results modules, and data density options
SpecsComponent specifications — annotated handoff documentation for engineering
07
Dashboard

Design Iterations

Iteration 1

Initial dashboard concept — establishing the query form and data output pattern

The dashboard is the centre of the platform — the main navigation hub and primary output surface for all user actions. The first iteration established the core layout: a persistent query form on the left, modular data output panels on the right.

Iteration 1Hi-fidelity dashboard — initial query form layout and data module system
User Testing Insight
First-time users signing in expected a more guided onboarding experience before reaching the main dashboard — the query form felt intimidating without context.
User Testing Insight
The query form felt too technical without inline examples. Users struggled to understand what level of precision was required for location and date range inputs.
Design Action
Introduced progressive disclosure in the query form and contextual help text — showing only essential fields by default with optional depth available on demand.
Design Action
Redesigned the empty state to guide first-time users through account setup before surfacing the dashboard — reducing confusion and building confidence from the first session.
Iteration 2

Refined for real users — onboarding, progressive disclosure, data confidence

The second iteration responded directly to usability findings. Progressive disclosure in the query form, improved empty states, and a reduced number of simultaneous data panels on initial load. We also introduced a data confidence indicator — a subtle addition that testing confirmed significantly improved user trust in the outputs.

Iteration 2Refined dashboard — progressive disclosure, onboarding flow, and data confidence indicators
08
Final Design

Production-Ready Output

The final design delivered a fully componentised Figma file with a custom design system, annotated handoff specs, and a pattern library the government department can extend independently. Designs met WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and were validated with a final round of usability testing before handoff.

The platform was recognised by the CDNA as an innovation in disaster preparedness design — cited for its approach to simplifying complex agricultural data for non-technical primary industry users.

Final DesignPlatform overview — production screens across dashboard, tools, and account management
Final DesignDashboard detail — final query form, results layout, and component system
MockupEnd-to-end platform — full-page view across desktop breakpoints
